talk_title: Technical implementation and early results of MedGamma fine‑tuning with QLoRA across consortium sites

speaker.name: Umut Topaloglu
speaker.affiliation: National Cancer Institute, Clinical and Translational Informatics Branch

MOA_summary: Umut described the federation of MedGamma using QLoRA adapters, where each site fine‑tunes a frozen foundation model on local lung and breast cancer records, then shares low‑rank weight updates via NVIDIA FLARE to produce a more robust multimodal extractor.
readouts: structured data extraction from pathology reports
key_findings: Three of four sites completed local QLoRA fine‑tuning, showing improved extraction accuracy versus out‑of‑the‑box MedGamma (visualized as orange vs blue bars).
One site demonstrated a statistically significant increase in variable extraction performance after fine‑tuning; other sites reported even larger gains.
Federated aggregation of QLoRA weights is scheduled, with the hypothesis that exposure to four centers’ data will further boost model robustness.
open_questions: What is the optimal level of differential privacy that balances privacy guarantees with model accuracy across heterogeneous sites?

talk_title: Fine‑tuning MedGamma with QLoRA for trial‑matching in lung and breast cancer cohorts

speaker.name: Ghulam Rasool
speaker.affiliation: Moffitt Cancer Center, Department of Machine Learning

MOA_summary: Rasool reported that local QLoRA fine‑tuning of MedGamma on ~500 retrospective lung and breast cancer patients per site markedly improved the model’s ability to extract structured trial‑matching variables from pathology reports and clinical notes, and that a forthcoming federated run will test whether pooled updates further enhance performance.
key_findings: Local fine‑tuning at one site increased extraction performance from baseline (blue) to post‑fine‑tuning (orange).
Two additional sites reported even larger improvements after local QLoRA adaptation.
Federated aggregation of QLoRA weights is planned for the week following the conference.
open_questions: Will federated QLoRA updates yield additive gains beyond the best local fine‑tuned model?

talk_title: Federated deep‑learning model for overall survival prediction in glioblastoma using MRIs

models.name: Vision Transformer with Brain SegFounder encoder
models.genotype_or_key_features: pre‑trained on healthy brain MRIs and BRATS glioma data

MOA_summary: Khanna described a federated pipeline where a pre‑trained Vision Transformer extracts 768‑dimensional embeddings from pre‑operative MRI tumor ROIs, which are pooled and fed to an MLP to predict relative overall‑survival risk; the model was trained across public GBM datasets and a real‑world Moffitt cohort (n=104) using NVIDIA FLARE with FedAvg aggregation.
key_findings: Added 104 real‑world Moffitt GBM patients to three public datasets without centralizing data.
Leave‑one‑site‑out evaluation showed consistent Kaplan‑Meier separation across held‑out cohorts.
Time‑dependent 1‑year AUC and C‑index were comparable to prior centralized studies, though absolute performance was not yet sufficient for clinical decision‑making.
Future plans include incorporating an additional private NCI GBM cohort and expanding to radiology reports, other imaging modalities, and clinical markers.
open_questions: How to accurately compute Cox partial likelihood in a federated setting given incomplete at‑risk information across sites?
What is the impact of batch‑wise approximation on survival model bias across heterogeneous cohorts?

cited_papers: AI‑RANO review in The Lancet Oncology
